item.title: Explainer: Why Iran’s move on closing the Strait of Hormuz matters
item.description: Tensions in the Strait of Hormuz have intensified, raising fears of a global energy shock as oil prices climb amid the escalating war between Iran, the United States and Israel. On Monday, an Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) commander declared the strait “closed” and warned tha

item.title: Low-cost bus, light rail options in focus
item.description: The new BNP government is likely to adopt a strategic transport policy centred on bus and light rail-based public transport in the capital, marking a major shift from the previous government's focus on mega-transport projects that largely failed to cover marginal commuters. However, officials and

item.title: Home Minister seeks Japan’s support in police reform
item.description: Home Minister Salahuddin Ahmed has sought Japan's cooperation in the reform and restructuring of the Bangladesh Police. The Minister requested this assistance when the Japanese Ambassador to Bangladesh, SAIDA Shinichi, called on him at his office at the Secretariat on Tuesday morning. The Ministe

item.title: Gold price rises again by Tk 3,324 per bhori
item.description: Gold prices have increased again in the country, with the rate of 22-carat gold rising by Tk 3,324 per bhori (11.664 grams), Bangladesh Jewellers Association (BAJUS) said on Tuesday. Following the latest adjustment, the price of 22-carat gold has been fixed at Tk 277,428 per bhori, according to a

item.title: Trade through Benapole land port suspended due to holiday in India
item.description: Trade between Bangladesh and India through Benapole Land Port remained suspended on Tuesday due to Dol Purnima holiday in India. Passport passenger movement between the two countries continued as usual despite the suspension of trade activities. Benapole Port Director Shamim Hossain said import-e

item.title: How can we attract foreign visitors to visit Bangladesh?
item.description: Bangladesh is blessed with a wide range of travel destinations, featuring breathtaking natural landscapes and archaeological heritages, that collectively represent the vast potential of the country's tourism sector. Cox's Bazar sea beach, coral islands, the hill tracts, mangrove forests, and pictu

item.title: Yunus declared himself 'VVIP' two days before polls
item.description: A government gazette issued on Feb 10, two days before the 13th national election, declared then interim government chief advisor Muhammad Yunus a “Very Very Important Person” (VVIP) for one year from the date of handing over office. The notification said the status was granted under S

item.title: Traffic speed rises in city as PM, top officials follow signals: Report
item.description: Compliance with traffic signals by the Prime Minister and other top state officials has contributed to an increase in vehicle speed on the capital's roads, according to a report submitted to Prime Minister Tarique Rahman today. "A report on the reduction of traffic congestion in the capital and th

item.title: NBR invites budget proposals for FY27
item.description: The National Board of Revenue (NBR) has invited budget proposals from taxpayers and key stakeholders as it begins preparations for 2026-27 fiscal year(FY27), aiming to frame a participatory and pro-people revenue policy. In a press release issued on Tuesday, the revenue authority said it regularly

item.title: Govt directs factories to clear February wages within first seven working days of March
item.description: The government on Tuesday instructed all factory owners to pay workers’ wages for February within the seven working days of March and to disburse festival allowances by March 12. Factory authorities have also been asked to pay half of the March wages in advance, following discussions between

item.title: ACC chairman, two commissioners resign
item.description: Anti-Corruption Commission (ACC) Chairman Dr MA and two commissioners resigned from their posts on Tuesday. The two commissioners are Brig Hafiz Ahsan Faridi and Mia Mohammad Ali Akbar Azizi. Sources at the ACC said the chairman and commissioners cited personal reasons in their resignation letter

item.title: Muktadir stresses bringing all sectors under a formal structure
item.description: Commerce Minister Khandaker Abdul Muktadir today (Tuesday) said that a large segment of Bangladesh's workforce still remains in the informal sector and stressed the need to gradually bring all sectors under a formal structure to ensure sustainable and balanced development. The Minister made the re
